projectDescription: A Claude Code plugin that delivers AI-powered venture thinking through Larry — a teaching partner that structures problem discovery, builds a persistent Data Room, and chains innovation methodologies using the PWS framework.
keyFeatures: Larry — AI thinking partner built on PWS methodology by Prof. Lawrence Aronhime
Data Room — persistent, auto-organized workspace that captures every decision, meeting, and finding
Intelligence cascade — every new entry is compared against all existing room content for contradictions, gaps, and connections
Opportunity Bank — grant and funding discovery with HSI scoring and deep research filing
Brain — optional remote Neo4j/Pinecone teaching graph for framework chaining and semantic search
70+ slash commands, 66 MCP tools, 8 specialized agents, 9 lifecycle hooks
Three surfaces — Claude Code CLI, Claude Desktop, and Cowork (multi-user)
Privacy-first — room data never leaves the user's machine; Brain queries carry methodology only
publicApiSurface: /mos:beautiful-question — reframe the problem before solving it
/mos:analyze-needs — analyze who has the problem and how badly
/mos:map-unknowns — surface the assumptions you are most confident about and wrong
/mos:research <url|topic> — web research wired as typed graph evidence
/mos:qualify-opportunity — judge a surfaced opportunity at a card
/mos:deep-grade — calibrated grading from 100+ real projects via Brain
/mos:pipeline — compose and run a framework chain
/mos:file-meeting — paste a transcript and auto-file it to the room
brain_ask MCP tool — guided DirectiveEnvelope from the teaching graph
brain_query MCP tool — read-only Cypher against the methodology graph
brain_search MCP tool — semantic vector search across teaching materials
brain_stats MCP tool — Brain index health and corpus counts
